title: Fiery Crispy Saudi Arabian Datterini Tomato Pilaf
description: A charred, crispy, nostalgic Saudi Arabian dish featuring datterini tomato, with an al dente finish and ayurvedic rajasic friendly ingredients
instructions: Heat the ghee in a large saucepan over medium heat
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning
Add the ground cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly
Add the halved datterini tomatoes and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until they start to char and release their juices
Add the basmati rice and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, until the rice is well coated with the spice mixture and starting to toast
Add 2 cups of water to the saucepan and bring to a boil
Reduce the heat to low, cover the saucepan with a tight fitting lid, and simmer for 15 minutes, or until the water is absorbed and the rice is cooked to an al dente finish
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it rest, covered, for 5 minutes
Fluff the pilaf with a fork and stir in the chopped fresh parsley
